DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO JUDGMENT DEBTORS KINOMEX MARKETS, INC. AND BRIAN FETTERS. KINOMEX MARKETS AND BRIAN FETTERS SHALL RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S POST JUDGMENT INTERROGATORIES AND PLAINTIFF'S POST JUDGMENT DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN 15 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. DENIED WITH RESPECT TO JUDGMENT DEBTOR ALBERT TOBIAS. THE PROOF OF SERVICE OF THE POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY ON MR. TOBIAS LUJAN STATES THAT THE POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY WAS SERVED BY MAIL, BUT DOES NOT REFLECT THAT THE DISCOVERY WAS SERVED UPON HIM AT HIS ADDRESS OF RECORD IN THIS ACTION. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. KINOMEX MARKETS SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF $170 WITHIN 15 DAYS. BRIAN FETTERS SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF $170 WITHIN 15 DAYS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: ERNST HALPERIN, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
Off Calendar Jun-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS.

Appearances: Douglas Provencher of Provencher & Flatt LLP (707.284.2380) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and moving party; no other appearances.

There being no other appearances, defendant is deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by a Judge Pro Tem. Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED with respect to judgment debtors Kinomex Markets, Inc. and Brian Fetters. Kinomex Markets and Brian Fetters shall respond to Plaintiff's Post Judgment Interrogatories and Plaintiff's Post Judgment Demand for Production of Documents without objection within 15 days of service of notice of entry of this order. DENIED with respect to judgment debtor Albert Tobias. The proof of service of the post-judgment discovery on Mr. Tobias Lujan states that the post-judgment discovery was served by mail, but does not reflect that the discovery was served upon him at his address of record in this action. Request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Kinomex Markets shall pay Plaintiff $170 within 15 days. Brian Fetters shall pay Plaintiff $170 within 15 days. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/JPT) Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin, not reported

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 501, Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not Reported

Attorney Elizabeth MacDonald appearing on behalf of plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc., dba Great Western Collection Bureau
Plaintiff's witness Carol Harris was sworn and examined.
No appearance by defendants Kimomex Markets, Inc., a California Corporation, dba Mercados Suvianda; Albert Tobias Lujan, Individually; Bryon Earl Fetters, Individually.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on July 13, 2011.
Doe defendants were dismissed on June 29, 2011.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc., dba Great Western Collection Bureau, and against defendants Kimomex Markets, Inc., a California Corporation, dba Mercados suvianda; Albert Tobias Lujan, Individually; Bryon Earl Fetters, Invidually as follows:

Damages - $5,221.65.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,888.06.
Attorney Fees - TBD by Motion.
Costs - $370.00

Total - $8,479.71

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
